content: Language education's teaching of reflexion as a deliberate vocabulary and critical thinking practice can be borrowed to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by framing structured self-reflection as a learnable, repeated linguistic skill for LLMs rather than just a ad-hoc computational module. Just as human students learn to intentionally reflect on their writing errors by explicitly practicing reflexion as a cognitive skill, LLMs can be fine-tuned to internalize consistent multi-perspective reflexion habits.
